personality_one_liner: A trustworthy, highly-structured calendar operations hub that feels like a polished Apple-native utility rather than a dry corporate dashboard.

spacing.rule: Generous Apple-native rhythms. Content is framed by negative space, not cramped. Forms and lists need comfortable vertical breathing room.

do's: Use glass for sticky bottom CTAs over scrolling forms.
Use glass for the bottom tab bar.
Apply glass to elevated sticky headers over history lists.
dont's: Do not use glass on standard form inputs or data list rows.
Do not blur entire background containers.

screens.name: Sign In
screens.purpose: OAuth entry point introducing the hub.

states.error: Standard alert or inline red banner above CTA.

screens.layout: Hero image at the top occupying 40% of the screen. Dark scrim over the image. A strong display title centered. A solid sticky primary CTA at the bottom 1/3 (thumb zone) triggering Google Sign-In.

screens.name: User Dashboard
screens.purpose: Shows the user's connection status and history of synced calendar events.

states.loading: Skeleton rows for the history list.
states.empty: A clean illustration (desk calendar) with 'No events synced yet. Waiting for admin broadcast.' and a soft refresh CTA.
states.error: Inline error banner 'Could not fetch history' with a Retry button.

screens.layout: Scrollable view. Top section: A prominent card displaying 'Google Account Linked' status with a success dot. Below: A section header 'Sync History' leading into a list of received events and notifications. Each row has a tinted icon container and an event title + timestamp.

screens.name: Admin Dashboard
screens.purpose: The command center for broadcasting events to all linked user calendars.

states.error: Form validation errors displayed directly under the respective inputs.

screens.layout: Top section: A segmented control or simple toggle acting as the 'Role Switcher' (Admin/User). Below: Event Creation Form (Title, Description, Start/End pickers) using single-column inputs with labels above. The 'Broadcast to All Users' CTA is fixed at the bottom in a glass container. Below the form (or in a separate swipeable tab/sheet), a list of past broadcasts showing delivery stats (X/Y users synced).

screens.name: Profile
screens.purpose: Account settings, notification preferences, and diagnostics.
screens.layout: Header with user's email/avatar. A list of settings rows (each with a tinted icon container) for FCM permission status, connection diagnostics, and a destructive row for Sign Out at the bottom.

history_empty.alt: Minimalist desk calendar showing empty schedule

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the No Blue/Purple rule; the app uses a rich Moss Green (brand) palette to feel trustworthy but native.
Implement the sticky primary CTA for the Admin Dashboard 'Broadcast' button using a fixed absolute container at the bottom, layered with a glass effect so scrolling form content goes beneath it.
Max font weight is 500 across all UI elements. Hierarchy must be built using size, not weight.
Always apply a vertical gradient scrim (transparent to surfaceInverse) over the auth_hero image to ensure the title overlay is fully readable.
Do NOT use pure black background for dark mode; use the specified #0D0D0D token.
Ensure the role switcher is clearly positioned at the top of the Home screen and exclusively visible if the user is identified as an Admin.
Every setting row in the Profile screen must use a tinted icon container (brandTertiary bg) rather than bare icons.
